meta_description: This article provides a comprehensive guide to CNC programming for beginners, introducing the basic concepts, structure and application of G-code and M-code, how to create G-code and M-code.

sections.heading: G and M code: a beginner's guide to CNC programming language
sections.text: G and M code: a beginner's guide to CNC programming language Published null, updated Dec 23, 2025 4 min CNC programming is a critical step in translating a design into an actual part. g-code and m-code are the basic languages used in CNC programming, controlling the geometric motion of the machine tool and auxiliary functions, respectively. G-Code: Controlling Machine Movement Basic G-Code Commands G00 - Rapid Positioning: Moves the tool quickly to the specified position without cutting. G01 - Linear Interpolation: Moves the tool along a straight line from one point to another at a specified feed rate to cut. G02 - Clockwise Arc Interpolation: Moves along a clockwise arc path to a specified...
